calculated by rule of three process.
6. TO ARRIVE AT NUMBER OF RAYS.
Note the position of the planet in Rasis, degrees etc., for which the number of rays is to be
computed. Deduct from this its (deep) debilitation point. If the remainder is in excess of six
Signs, reduce it again from 12. Multiply the product by the maximum number of rays allotted
to the planet concerned and divide by six. The resultant figure will represent the number of
rays obtained by the planet by virtue of its position at birth.
7-9. RECTIFICATION OF RAYS.
If the planet is in the dwadasamsa of its friend, double the product, as obtained above. If it is in its
own dwadasamsa, own Rasi, retrogression/exaltation Sign the product should be trebled. If it
is in its enemys dwadasamsa, 1/16th of the rays will be lost. The same applies to a debilitated
planet. A combust planet, excepting Venus and Saturn, loses all the rays. A planet, that has
